!!! THIS FILE IS A TEST SNAPSHOT — NOT SOURCE CODE !!!

It contains the expected output of the LogExpr codegen (main.rs) run
against function-catalog.yaml. It is compared by the codegen_snapshot
test. If you change the catalog or the codegen, run:

    cargo test -- codegen_snapshot

and update this file with the new output if the diff is intentional.

Hash lines (CATALOG_HASH, CODEGEN_HASH, EXPR_GEN_HASH) are excluded
from comparison because they change whenever the codegen source is
touched.

--- EXPECTED OUTPUT BELOW THIS LINE ---
// Generated from function-catalog.yaml — do not edit manually.
// Re-generate with:
//   cargo run -q -p meta-codegen -- function-catalog.yaml

use crate::value::Value;

/// Combined hash of the catalog + codegen that produced this file.
/// Consumers that pattern-match on generated types should assert
/// against this constant to detect silent schema drift.

/// Generated — do not modify. See top of file.
/// Logical expression tree — value-based, fully recursive.
///
/// Used for normalization and rewrite rules where nested
/// pattern matching is the natural style.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum LogExpr {
    Literal(Value),

    LogicalOr { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    LogicalAnd { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    LogicalNot { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// both params must unify. null input → null output. See null_safe_equal for SQL IS NOT DISTINCT FROM.
    Equal { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// both params must unify. null input → null output. See null_safe_not_equal for SQL IS DISTINCT FROM.
    NotEqual { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    LessThan { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    LessOrEqual { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    GreaterThan { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    GreaterOrEqual { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// no CEL equivalent; lower to LogicalAnd(GreaterOrEqual(x, lo), LessOrEqual(x, hi))
    Between { arg0: Box<LogExpr>, arg1: Box<LogExpr>, arg2: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// SQL null-safe equality (null == null → true). Distinct from Equal which propagates null.
    NullSafeEqual { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// SQL null-safe inequality (null != null → false). Distinct from NotEqual which propagates null.
    NullSafeNotEqual { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// CEL uses has() macro for presence; we likely need this for nullable columns
    IsNull { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    IsNotNull { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// useful for float column guards
    IsNan { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    IsFinite { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// NaN is neither finite nor infinite; IsInfinite != LogicalNot(IsFinite)
    IsInfinite { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// returns first non-null argument
    Coalesce { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Least { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Greatest { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// also string/bytes/list concat and timestamp+duration in CEL
    Add { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Subtract { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Multiply { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Divide { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Negate { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Modulus { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Abs { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Power { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Sqrt { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Exp { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Sign { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Contains { receiver: Box<LogExpr>, arg: Box<LogExpr> },
    StartsWith { receiver: Box<LogExpr>, arg: Box<LogExpr> },
    EndsWith { receiver: Box<LogExpr>, arg: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// CEL returns bool; Substrait returns the matched substring
    RegexMatch { receiver: Box<LogExpr>, arg: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// also maps to Substrait octet_length for bytes, and list/map cardinality
    Size { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Lower { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Upper { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Substring { arg0: Box<LogExpr>, arg1: Box<LogExpr>, arg2: Box<LogExpr> },
    Replace { arg0: Box<LogExpr>, arg1: Box<LogExpr>, arg2: Box<LogExpr> },
    Trim { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    StringSplit { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    StringPosition { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// in CEL this is the _+_ operator on strings
    Concat { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// CEL splits into per-component methods; we unify as extract(component, ts)
    TimestampExtract { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// useful for time bucketing in predicates
    RoundTemporal { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    RoundCalendar { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// maps to coeffect reads_current_time in our model
    CurrentTimestamp,
    CastBool { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    CastInt { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    CastUint { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    CastDouble { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    CastString { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    CastBytes { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    CastDuration { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    CastTimestamp { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    TypeOf { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// type-checker hint only; no runtime effect
    Dyn { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Conditional { condition: Box<LogExpr>, then_expr: Box<LogExpr>, else_expr: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// also works as map(K, V)[K] → V; overloaded
    Index { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    In { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    Ln { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Log10 { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Ceil { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Floor { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    Round { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// Parse a JSON string into a dynamically-typed value. Returns Any; caller must cast to a concrete type. JSON arrays become Array[Any], JSON objects become Map[String, Any].
    JsonParse { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// Parse a JSON object string into a StructType. Field names in the JSON become struct field names; types are inferred or declared by schema. The common case: a varchar column containing an escaped JSON document. Unlike JsonParse, the target struct type must be known at compile time.
    JsonParseStruct { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// serialize any value to a JSON string
    JsonStringify { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// Extract a value from a JSON string by JSONPath-like expression. First param is the JSON string, second is the path (e.g. "$.shipping.country"). Returns Any; avoids full parse when only one field is needed.
    JsonExtract { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// Like JsonExtract but always returns the result as a string, even for numbers/booleans. Matches Trino's json_extract_scalar behavior.
    JsonExtractString { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// Tests whether an IP address (first param) falls within a CIDR block (second param, e.g. "10.0.0.0/8"). Supports both IPv4 and IPv6. Addresses are strings; we may introduce a native IP type later.
    CidrContains { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// Tests whether an IP address matches any CIDR block in a list. Sugar for exists(cidrs, c, CidrContains(ip, c)) but evaluable with a radix tree for large block lists.
    CidrMatch { lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// parse IPv4 to u32 or IPv6 to u128; useful for range comparisons
    IpToInt { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    /// render integer as dotted-quad (v4) or colon-hex (v6)
    IntToIp { operand: Box<LogExpr> },

    GetFieldByName { field_name: String },
    GetFieldByIndex { field_index: u32 },
    GetChildByName { child_name: String, operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    GetChildByIndex { child_index: u32, lhs: Box<LogExpr>, rhs: Box<LogExpr> },
    GetColumn { column_id: String },

    Has { operand: Box<LogExpr> },
    All { collection: Box<LogExpr>, binding: String, body: Box<LogExpr> },
    Exists { collection: Box<LogExpr>, binding: String, body: Box<LogExpr> },
    ExistsOne { collection: Box<LogExpr>, binding: String, body: Box<LogExpr> },
    Filter { collection: Box<LogExpr>, binding: String, body: Box<LogExpr> },
    MapTransform { collection: Box<LogExpr>, binding: String, body: Box<LogExpr> },

    /// Multi-way conditional (desugared CASE).
    Case { arms: Vec<(Box<LogExpr>, Box<LogExpr>)>, default: Box<LogExpr> },
}
